popularStory.headline: Arnold I (approx 1050-approx 1130)
popularStory.description:
Arnold I (approx 1050-approx 1130), Count of Looz (Loon) (from approx 1079), son of Emmo, Count of Loon, and Suanhildis, daughter of Dirk III, Count of Holland, and his wife Othelandis.

He was an all …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Arnold I GRAAF VAN LOON於大約1060年出生在比利時利包格波格洛時，他的父親Emmo VAN LOON41歲，母親Swanhilde van Holland39歲。他和Agnes von Mainz-Rieneck至少有1個兒子。他去世於1135年享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Arnold I GRAAF VAN LOON于大约1060年出生在比利时利包格波格洛时，他的父亲Emmo VAN LOON41岁，母亲Swanhilde van Holland39岁。他和Agnes von Mainz-Rieneck至少有1个儿子。他去世于1135年享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: VAN
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: LOON
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: variant of   Mohan  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Moyon in La Manche. Compare   Mounce  .
Americanized form of German   Mohn   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Mohan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ram, Krishna, Madan, Chandra, Brij, Rakesh, Vivek, Anil, Chander, Leela, Raj, Sanjay.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mocháin ‘descendant of Mochán’, a personal name from a diminutive of moch ‘early, timely’. In Connacht this name was changed to Mahon ; it is also found translated into English as Early . It has been used to represent the Norman habitational name de Mohun.

Indian (Punjab; also southern states): from a personal name based on Sanskrit mohana ‘fascinating, enchanting’, an epithet of the god Krishna. In the Punjab it is a Brahmin name, borne by a clan in the Mohyal subgroup of Saraswat Brahmins; it probably evolved from an ancestral personal name.

variants.name: Moone
meanings: Irish and English: variant of   Moon  .

variants.name: Mounce
meanings: English (Devon; of Norman origin): habitational name, possibly from Moyon (Manche). Compare   Moon   2.
Possibly an Americanized form of German and Swiss German   Manz  .

variants.name: Mohun
meanings: English: variant of   Moon  .
Irish: variant of   Mohan  .

nameMeanings.name: Arnold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  Jewish: Yiddish contracted form of   Aaron .
2  English: short form of   Arnold .

nameMeanings.name: GRAAF
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graf
meanings: See  Grey .

en.lifeSummary: When Arnold I GRAAF VAN LOON was born about 1060, in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgium, his father, Emmo VAN LOON, was 41 and his mother, Swanhilde van Holland, was 39. He had at least 1 son with Agnes von Mainz-Rieneck. He died in 1135, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Arnold I GRAAF VAN LOON est né environ 1060, à Borgloon, Limbourg, Belgique, son père, Emmo VAN LOON, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Swanhilde van Holland, avait 39 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Agnes von Mainz-Rieneck. Il est décédé en 1135, à l’âge de 76 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Arnold I GRAAF VAN LOON um 1060 in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Emmo VAN LOON 41 und seine Mutter Swanhilde van Holland 39. Er hatte mit Agnes von Mainz-Rieneck mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1135 im Alter von 76 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Arnold I GRAAF VAN LOON è nato il circa 1060 nel Borgloon, Limburgo, Belgio, il suo padre, Emmo VAN LOON, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Swanhilde van Holland, aveva 39 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Agnes von Mainz-Rieneck. Morì nel 1135, all'età di 76 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Arnold I GRAAF VAN LOONが約1060年にベルギーリンブルグボルグロオンで生まれた時，父Emmo VAN LOONは41歳，母Swanhilde van Hollandは39歳であった。彼はAgnes von Mainz-Rieneckとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1135年に76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Arnold I GRAAF VAN LOON(이)가 대략 1060년 벨기에 림버그 볼그루에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Emmo VAN LOON(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Swanhilde van Holland(은)는 39세였다. 그는 Agnes von Mainz-Rieneck(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1135년 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Arnold I GRAAF VAN LOON nasceu aproximadamente 1060, em Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica, seu pai, Emmo VAN LOON, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Swanhilde van Holland, tinha 39 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Agnes von Mainz-Rieneck. Ele faleceu em 1135, com 76 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Arnold I GRAAF VAN LOON родился около 1060 г. в местности борглун, лимбург, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Emmo VAN LOON было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Swanhilde van Holland, 39. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Agnes von Mainz-Rieneck. Он умер в 1135 г. в возрасте 76 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Arnold I GRAAF VAN LOON nació aproximadamente 1060, en Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica, su papá, Emmo VAN LOON, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Swanhilde van Holland, tenía 39 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Agnes von Mainz-Rieneck. Murió en 1135, a la edad de 76 años.
